title: Tapeinia squalida
taxonomy: Tapeinia squalida is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Tapeinia squalida is native to Australia and is found in the eastern and southern regions of the country. The genus Tapeinia is relatively small and contains a few species of flowering plants that are native to Australia. Tapeinia squalida is a plant that is often found in woodland and open forest areas.

intro: Tapeinia squalida, also known as the 'Australian Sandpaper Bush', is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia, characterized by its unique, sandpapery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various skin conditions and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Tapeinia squalida include squalilactone and other compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as creams, ointments, and salves to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
